title: Modular Glass Rack
summary: A great compromise of form and function.
description: A great compromise of form and function. This Pottery Barn inspired glass rack, is part of a modular set that can be used in combination with other modular pieces (see image 2), or it can be used alone!

steps.title: Frame
steps.body: Join both 25 1/2" and 24" 1x6" boards using wood glue and 2 inch, 16 gauge finishing nails. This will form a 25 1/2" square. Secure using glue and 2" 16 gauge finishing nails.

steps.title: Shelves
steps.body: Place cut out 1"x 6"x 24" so that the base of the board is 3" from the top of the unit. Secure with wood glue, and 2" 16 gauge nails. Place 1"x 6"x 24" so that the base of the board is 12" from the top of the unit. Secure with wood glue, and 2" 16 gauge nails. Place 1"x 6"x 24" so that the base of the board is 18" from the top of the unit. Secure with wood glue, and 2" 16 gauge nails.

steps.title: Front Rails
steps.body: Attach the 25 1/2" 1x2" board(s) to the face of the unit using wood glue and 2 inch 16 gauge finishing nails.

steps.title: Back Support
steps.body: Attach the remaining 24" 1x2"board using wood glue and 2 inch 16 gauge finishing nails. This is an ideal place to attach the unit to the wall, or you can attach hanging hardware to the back.

steps.title: Glass Rack Cut Out
steps.body: The glass rack cutout will be made out of one of the 1"x 6" x 24" boards. Use a jigsaw to cut out 1' wide and 3" deep cut outs. Use the dimensions on the diagram to find the center to center placement of the cutouts.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill all nail holes with wood filler. Sand smooth, then apply desire stain and/or paint.
